channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇵🇭🇨🇳 Philippines protests China's floating structure on disputed South China Sea shoal
item.description: Manila has protested Beijing's deployment of a floating structure with personnel on Scarborough Shoal, a contested feature in the South China Sea. The move escalates tensions in the strategically vital waterway.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi cements sway over North Korea as Kim names China top priority
item.description: Kim Jong Un feted Chinese President Xi Jinping with lavish celebrations in Pyongyang, calling ties with China a strategic priority. The visit underscores deepening alignment between Beijing and Pyongyang amid regional tensions.

item.title: ⚖️ ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan suspended over sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: In an unprecedented move, the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court was suspended from his duties pending a decision by an oversight body on sexual misconduct allegations.

item.title: 📊⛽ Trump approval near record low as Americans expect higher gas prices
item.description: Only 22% of Americans approve of how President Trump is managing the cost of living, with most expecting higher gasoline prices amid Middle East tensions affecting global oil markets.

item.title: 📈💰 Investors see tipping point for bonds after Iran conflict shreds old playbooks
item.description: Bonds failed to shield investors during the Iran-Israel conflict, but fund managers say debt may reclaim its safe-haven role if inflation starts hitting growth, marking a potential shift in market dynamics.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇮🇷 Iran Confirms Two Military Personnel Killed in Israeli Strikes
item.description: Iranian state media reports two high-ranking air defense personnel killed in Israeli strikes on Monday, as diplomatic sources caution that a deal to end the US-Iran conflict within days is highly unlikely.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Israeli Ministers Call for Bombing Beirut Over Hezbollah
item.description: Senior Israeli ministers call for striking the Lebanese capital and detaining women in retaliation against Hezbollah, escalating rhetoric amid ongoing cross-border conflict.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇨🇳 Secret Camera Found in UK Office Where China Embassy Approved
item.description: A hidden camera was discovered in the Whitehall building where UK ministers approved a new Chinese embassy, raising security concerns at a sensitive time for UK-China relations.

item.title: 🇫🇮☢️ Finland Considers Allowing Nuclear Weapons Import in Crises
item.description: Finlands parliamentary defence committee backs legislation to allow nuclear weapons imports during crisis situations, marking a significant shift for the traditionally non-nuclear Nordic nation.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇦🇫 EU to Discuss Deporting Afghan Refugees with Taliban
item.description: European countries are preparing to discuss deportation agreements with the Taliban government for Afghan refugees, a controversial move as the bloc faces migration pressures.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US blacklists Alibaba, Baidu, BYD as military-linked Chinese companies
item.description: The Pentagon added dozens of Chinese companies including tech giant Alibaba, EV maker BYD, and search engine Baidu to its list of firms allegedly aiding Beijing's military, a move Beijing condemned as discriminatory.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping wraps up rare North Korea visit with deeper cooperation pledge
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping completed his first visit to North Korea in seven years, establishing a deeper understanding and clearer path for development of ties between the two countries.

item.title: ⚛️🇮🇷 IAEA urges Iran to re-engage as West pushes nuclear resolution
item.description: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to resume inspections at sites bombed by the US and Israel, as Washington led efforts for a resolution at the agency's board meeting.

item.title: ⚽🇮🇷 Iran World Cup ticket allocation revoked days before tournament
item.description: Iran's football federation said FIFA revoked its ticket allocation for fans at World Cup games in the US, leaving supporters with travel plans stranded.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Maine Senate primary under scrutiny over candidate's past
item.description: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner faces voter scrutiny over online posts, a Nazi-linked tattoo, and explicit text allegations in a race that could determine party control of the US Senate.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China prepares $295 billion plan for nationwide AI infrastructure buildout
item.description: China is preparing to spend around 2 trillion yuan over five years on data centers across the country to challenge the US in the AI race, as geopolitical tensions drive technology competition.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskyy arrives in Estonia for Nordic-Baltic summit on Ukraine support
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is in Estonia to attend a summit of Nordic and Baltic leaders, seeking continued military and financial support as Ukraine presses its counteroffensive against Russia.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German exports rise unexpectedly in April despite Iran war headwinds
item.description: German exports rose by 0.9% in April compared to the previous month, defying analyst expectations of a decline amid tensions in the Middle East and global supply chain disruptions.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump confirms US Army Apache helicopter crew safe after incident near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said the pilots of a US Army Apache helicopter that went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz are safe, amid ongoing tensions between Iran and Israel.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Microsoft Cuts Hundreds of Cloud Jobs in China as Data Laws Tighten
item.description: Microsoft is laying off employees at its China cloud unit amid ongoing restrictions on cross-border data flows between Beijing and Washington, reflecting growing tech tensions.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China Firmly Opposes US Blacklisting of Alibaba, Baidu and Other Firms
item.description: Beijing condemned Washington's decision to blacklist major Chinese companies including Alibaba and Baidu, vowing to defend its interests.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇱🇧 Pakistan and Lebanon Army Chiefs Meet as Regional Mediation Continues
item.description: The heads of the Pakistani and Lebanese armed forces agreed to boost cooperation during talks in Pakistan as Middle East diplomatic efforts drag on.

item.title: 🌍 Global Conflicts Reach Highest Level Since World War II
item.description: A new report shows global conflicts surged in 2025, reaching levels not seen since World War II, as simultaneous wars in Ukraine, Middle East, and other regions strain international institutions.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Contractor Scraps $800 Million IPO Citing Iran War Uncertainty
item.description: Saudi Arabia's Mutlaq Al-Ghowairi Contracting Company cancelled its planned IPO in Riyadh that could have raised up to $800 million, marking one of the first regional offerings shelved since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Recaptures Over 600 Square Kilometers in 2026
item.description: Ukraine's military chief says Kyiv has retaken more than 600 square kilometers of territory in 2026, with independent battlefield mappers confirming Russia's advances have slowed or reversed in recent months.
